title: Spermacoce tenuis
intro: Spermacoce tenuis, also known as slender bee-balm or slender false buttonweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it useful in treating fever, digestive issues, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Spermacoce tenuis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Spermacoce tenuis is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, making it a common ingredient in traditional remedies for various health conditions.
taxonomy: Spermacoce tenuis is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is part of the order Gentianales, which includes various species of plants that have similar characteristics and growth habits. Spermacoce tenuis is further grouped into the genus Spermacoce, a group of plants that are often referred to as bugle herbs or witchweed. The genus Spermacoce is part of the tribe Spermacoceae, a group of plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Spermacoce tenuis is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as more information becomes available.
